behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: The behavior has two requirements: (1) validation rejects invalid manifests, and (2) no plugin code executes. A Unit test of the validator alone would miss the loader executing plugin code despite validation. Integration testing with actual manifest files and the full loader ensures both the validation and the execution-prevention work together.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin directory with a manifest.json missing a required field (e.g., missing 'name'). Load the plugins and verify: (a) the loader reports a validation error, (b) the plugin's entry module is never imported or executed.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's onLoad hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: Mocking module imports would hide the real failure: plugin A's import-time code executes before B is initialized. The real failure boundary is code that runs at module import time (not just in onLoad). Must use actual plugin files to test real Node.js module import ordering and initialization.
behaviors.test_description: Create two real plugin files: Plugin B (no dependencies) that sets a marker during import, and Plugin A (declares B as dependency) whose onLoad verifies the marker exists. Load plugins and verify: (a) B's module import completes before A's module import, (b) B's onLoad is called before A's onLoad.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: While cycle detection is pure graph logic, the spec requires 'loads neither'—verifying that the loader doesn't attempt to execute cyclic plugins despite detecting the cycle. A Unit test of the algorithm could pass while the loader still tries to load them. Integration testing verifies the complete behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create two plugin manifest.json files where A depends on B and B depends on A. Load the plugins and verify: (a) the loader detects and reports the circular dependency, (b) neither plugin's onLoad hook is called.

behaviors.description: A plugin's onLoad hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is when onLoad runs before the module's import-time initialization code, causing undefined references. Unit tests with mocked modules would hide actual Node.js import execution timing. Must use real plugin files to test the real import/hook ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin file that sets a global marker during module import. The plugin's onLoad reads this marker; if not set, onLoad fails. Load the plugin and verify the marker is set before onLoad is called, confirming import executed first.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its onLoad hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Testing 'remaining plugins that do not depend on it' requires a multi-plugin dependency graph with real errors. Unit tests with mocked hooks might miss failures like partial initialization of dependent plugins before error handling kicks in. Integration testing with real plugins and dependencies catches these complex scenarios.
behaviors.test_description: Create three plugins: A (no dependencies), B (depends on A, onLoad throws), and C (depends on B). Load all three and verify: (a) A loads and onLoad is called, (b) B's onLoad throws and error is reported, (c) C does not load (since B failed).
